pages.full_text:
November 5th.  2016
Simon, Eve & Tinder left 86 just after 9. The van has only done 1100 miles this year instead of the usual 3000. We went to Japan instead of France. We stopped at Inigo Jones' windmill for a walk. Then on to Forge Ho[use] Gallery, opened by Sheena Rogerson today. We bought "Bridget things."
Gorgeous autumn colours. There's been very little rain. We had lunch in the carpark at Charlecote Park. Lucy family from 1066. V. nice, welcoming, red brick! Beer brewing. monuments in church. Bedrooms, inlaid tables.

Now on our way home.
pages.notes: Page contains a Charlecote Park branded sticker/badge (circular, red with logo). 'Forge Ho' appears abbreviated at line end — likely 'Forge House'. The text 'Lucy family from 1066' likely refers to the Lucy family who owned Charlecote Park. 'carpark' may read 'carpark' or 'car park'. 'Bridget things' appears in quotes in the original.

pages.full_text:
April 9th  Palm Sunday  2017
     We drove to Tolkein's area & parked outside Rosie's Rd, Keel Drive, next to a field. Alas, she was away. Betty, Walter, Olive & au pair Paola, were with us. (Thomas & Naomi are taking Austin & Flora up Ben Nevis.).
A lovely afternoon, the children climbing up sloping tree trunks. Near Sarehole Mill.

April 22nd Saturday
     We left home at 11, reached the Green Man & had to circle back to get the bread out of the bread crock.
     Thomas is managing Chad & doing music. William is putting machinery in his hop barn. Peter is doing a law conversion. John has finished doctoring in Bristol, is building a Caterham at the farm & about to train in anaesthetics in Cardiff. Charles is getting investors for his robot company. His robot pinned him to the wall at the farm earlier this year.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 96 visible at top right corner. 'Tolkein's area' refers to J.R.R. Tolkien's childhood area in Birmingham, near Sarehole Mill. 'Rosie's Rd, Keel Drive' may refer to a road named after Tolkien's character; could also read 'Keel Drive' as 'Keel Drive' — reasonably clear. 'Green Man' is likely a pub name. 'Caterham' refers to a Caterham kit car. April 22nd 2017 was indeed a Saturday, supporting the date confidence. The entry at the top of the right page is a continuation from a different date entry than the left page — the left page covers November 2016 and the right page begins April 2017.
